subject: Product Management
title: The Innovator's Dilemma
description: A 60-second lesson on The Innovator's Dilemma.

hook.text: Christensen's greatest insight: doing everything right can still lead to failure when the market shifts underneath you.

buildup.text: Established companies listen to customers, invest in quality, and improve margins — all 'right' moves.

discovery.text: But disruptive products start worse and cheaper, serving overlooked segments. They improve until they overtake incumbents.

twist.text: The dilemma: your best customers don't want the disruptive product, so rational analysis says ignore it.

climax.text: Kodak, Blockbuster, and Nokia all fell to disruption while doing exactly what their current customers wanted.

punchline.text: Serving today's customers perfectly can blind you to tomorrow's market.

quiz.question: What is the innovator's dilemma?
options: Doing everything right for current customers can make you miss disruptive shifts
Innovating too fast and confusing customers
Spending too much on research and development
